claim: MCP (Model Context Protocol) enables dynamic discovery of agent capabilities at runtime without redeploying code
actionable: Design agents using MCP to query server capabilities (tools/list, resources/list) dynamically and adapt workflows without redeployment
source_title: MCP vs API: Simplifying AI Agent Integration with External Data

claim: MCP standardizes interfaces across all servers (same protocol and patterns), whereas each REST API requires unique adapters
actionable: Prefer MCP over REST APIs when building multi-tool agents to reduce integration complexity and enable tool discovery at runtime
source_title: MCP vs API: Simplifying AI Agent Integration with External Data

claim: MCP servers often wrap existing REST APIs internally, translating MCP format to native API calls
actionable: Understand that MCP is a standardization layer; implement MCP servers as wrappers around existing APIs rather than replacing them
source_title: MCP vs API: Simplifying AI Agent Integration with External Data

claim: MCP supports three primitives: tools (discrete actions), resources (read-only data), and prompt templates (predefined prompts)
actionable: Structure external integrations as one or more of these three primitive types when designing MCP servers
source_title: MCP vs API: Simplifying AI Agent Integration with External Data

claim: MCP provides standard client-server architecture where clients open JSON RPC 2.0 sessions to connect external servers
actionable: Use JSON RPC 2.0 communication when building MCP servers to ensure compatibility with MCP clients
source_title: MCP vs API: Simplifying AI Agent Integration with External Data
